rulings.Case Title: EMIL MICHAEL VS. JOHN DANFORTH ET AL
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Motion And Motion For Sanctions Pursuant To Ccp 128.7
rulings.Rulings: Real Property/Housing Court Law and Motion Calendar for Thursday, December 17, 2015, line 3. DEFENDANT JOHN DANFORTH's Motion For Sanctions Pursuant To Ccp 128.7 DENIED. Moving party failed to comply with the safe harbor provision and failed to serve the Plaintiff with a motion for sanctions (including notice of motion, motion, points and authorities and supporting evidence) 21 days before the motion was filed. Parties' requests for sanctions are denied. = (501/REQ)

rulings.Case Title: CARLOS ABELA VS. VARUN SINGHAL ET AL
rulings.Rulings: Real Property/Housing Court Law and Motion Calendar for Thursday, December 17, 2015, line 4. DEFENDANT VARUN SINGHAL's DEMURRER TO COMPLAINT SUSTAINED in part and OVERRULED in part. Demurrer is overruled as to the first cause of action for breach of contract as it may be ascertained from he complaint that the operative contract is written. Demurrer is sustained with leave to amend as to the fourth cause of action for plaintiff to allege facts in support of each element of cause of action for conversion. Demurer to the fifth cause of action is sustained without leave to amend. Plaintiff fails to cite any authority for the proposition that Cal. Constitution Article X, section 2 is applicable to the facts of this case. = (501/REQ)

rulings.Calendar Matter: Amended Notice Of Motion And Motion To Strike Under C.C.P. Section 425.16
rulings.Rulings: Real Property/Housing Court Law and Motion Calendar for Thursday, December 17, 2015, line 6. DEFENDANTS LELAND RYAN, DWIGHT DONOVAN and FOX ROTHSCHILD LLP's Amended Motion To Strike Under C.C.P. Section 425.16 DENIED. Moving party failed to sustain its burden under the first prong and show that the alleged causes of action based on unlawful lockout, conversion of personal property, etc., arise out of protected activity within the meaning of CCP 425.16. = (501/REQ)

rulings.Case Title: BIANA CHERNOGUZ VS. GUILLERMO MANZANARES ET AL
rulings.Rulings: Real Property/Housing Court Law and Motion Calendar for Thursday, December 17, 2015, line 15. Defendant'S Motion For Summary Judgment GRANTED. Moving party shifted the burned and Opposing party failed to create a triable issue of fact with competent admissible evidence regarding the "no sublet" term of the oral rental agreement between Defendant and Plaintiff's predecessor in interest. Also see Foster v. Britton, 2015 WL 7732634.= (501/REQ)
